instruction: Generate a 3D low-poly glossy blue cube icon.
output:
import bpy

# Clear default objects
bpy.ops.object.select_all(action='SELECT')
bpy.ops.object.delete(use_global=False)

# Add a cube
bpy.ops.mesh.primitive_cube_add(size=2, enter_editmode=False, align='WORLD', location=(0, 0, 0))
cube = bpy.context.active_object

# Create a shiny blue material
mat = bpy.data.materials.new(name="GlossyBlue")
mat.use_nodes = True
nodes = mat.node_tree.nodes
principled = nodes.get("Principled BSDF")
principled.inputs['Base Color'].default_value = (0.0, 0.2, 0.8, 1.0)
principled.inputs['Roughness'].default_value = 0.1
cube.data.materials.append(mat)

# Export to GLTF format
bpy.ops.export_scene.gltf(filepath="output.gltf", export_format='GLTF_EMBEDDED')
